skill.name: new-agent-creation
skill.description: Guides developers through creating Unite-Hub AI agents with templates for implementation, registration, tests, and governance checks.
skill.summary: Create Unite-Hub AI agents with implementation, registration, testing, and governance guidance.
skill.author: CleanExpo

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged Markdown backticks, a relative import, and weak-cryptography patterns in SKILL.md. Review found no executable shell commands, cryptographic API use, data exfiltration, or prompt-injection attack text. The main residual concern is that the example agent forwards task payloads to a model without explicit validation guidance.

medium_findings.title: Example Agent Sends Raw Task Payload To Model
medium_findings.description: The template builds an Anthropic message from task.task_type and task.payload. This is legitimate sample code, but production agents should validate payloads and add prompt-injection controls.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The code example clearly forwards task data to a model. The risk depends on how developers adapt the template, so this is a moderate concern rather than confirmed malicious behavior.

low_findings.title: Static External Command Matches Are Markdown Formatting
low_findings.description: The flagged Ruby or shell backtick patterns appear in Markdown inline code, fenced code blocks, and a TypeScript template literal. I did not find evidence of command execution in SKILL.md.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Each location is part of documentation or example code formatting. No shell, Ruby, child process, or command invocation is present.

low_findings.title: Relative Import Is Not Path Traversal
low_findings.description: The path traversal hit is a static relative import in an optional JavaScript runner example. It does not read files, write files, or include user-controlled path input.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The evidence is a fixed import path inside an example code block. There is no dynamic path construction or filesystem operation at the flagged line.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Matches Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The flagged weak cryptography locations do not contain hash functions, ciphers, or cryptographic configuration. They are ordinary description text and TypeScript example syntax.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed lines contain no MD5, SHA1, DES, RC4, or similar algorithm use. The duplicate line 3 hit is scanner noise from the skill description.

dangerous_patterns.title: Unvalidated LLM Prompt Construction In Example Code
dangerous_patterns.description: The example constructs a model prompt from task metadata and payload. This pattern should be paired with validation and clear trust boundaries in production agents.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The prompt construction is visible in the sample. It is a design risk if copied directly, not evidence of malware in the skill.

content.value_statement: Teams need a repeatable process for adding Unite-Hub agents. This skill provides templates for agent classes, routing, registry entries, tests, and governance checks.
actual_capabilities: Shows a TypeScript BaseAgent class template for a new Unite-Hub agent.
Explains where to add orchestrator intent routing for the new agent.
Provides a registry entry structure for agent metadata and governance settings.
Includes a Vitest-style test template for basic task processing checks.
Documents an optional CLI runner pattern for starting an agent process.
Lists setup checklist items for budgets, verification, and workspace filtering.
limitations: The guidance is specific to Unite-Hub project conventions.
It does not generate files or update a repository automatically.
Example code needs adaptation before production use.
Security controls are checklist guidance, not automated enforcement.

use_cases.title: Add a New Product Agent
use_cases.description: Create a new agent class, routing intent, registry entry, and basic tests for a product workflow.

use_cases.title: Standardize Agent Governance
use_cases.description: Use the checklist to document budgets, verification requirements, and workspace boundaries before launch.

use_cases.title: Review Agent Onboarding Steps
use_cases.description: Compare a proposed agent implementation against expected files, tests, and registration steps.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Basic Agent Plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the new-agent-creation skill to outline the files I need for a Unite-Hub agent named CustomerFollowUpAgent.

prompt_templates.title: Draft Agent Registration Steps
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the skill to help me register an agent for invoice review, including routing intent, registry fields, and governance settings.

prompt_templates.title: Build a Testing Checklist
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the skill to create a focused test checklist for a new agent that processes workspace-scoped tasks.

prompt_templates.title: Review a Production Agent Design
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the skill to review my planned Unite-Hub agent design for missing files, routing gaps, budget controls, and verification requirements.

output_examples.input: Plan a new agent for handling partner onboarding tasks.
output_examples.output: A concise implementation plan with agent file location, routing update, registry fields, tests, and governance checklist items.

output_examples.input: Check whether my new agent is ready for review.
output: Agent class extends the expected base class.
Routing and registry updates are listed.
Tests, budget limits, and verification checks are identified.

output_examples.input: Help define governance settings for a marketing agent.
output_examples.output: A review note covering human governance, daily budget, verification requirements, and workspace filtering expectations.

best_practices: Validate task payloads before sending them to a model.
Add tests for workspace filtering, retries, and failure handling.
Set governance mode and budget limits before enabling the agent.
anti_patterns: Do not copy the sample agent without adapting validation and error handling.
Do not register an agent before its routing and tests are complete.
Do not use autonomous governance without clear approval and budget controls.

faq.question: What does this skill help me create?
faq.answer: It helps you create a Unite-Hub AI agent with implementation, routing, registry, tests, and governance steps.

faq.question: Does the skill create files automatically?
faq.answer: No. It provides templates and guidance that a developer applies in the project.

faq.question: Which tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Is this skill safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes, with a medium-risk warning about validating payloads before model calls.

faq.question: Why were static security findings reported?
faq.answer: The scanner matched Markdown backticks, code fences, and a relative import in documentation examples.

faq.question: What should developers review before production use?
faq.answer: They should review payload validation, prompt-injection controls, tests, budgets, and workspace isolation.
